title: Training-Free vs Probe-Based JRN: Diagnostic Comparison on rel-f1
summary: Demo notebook comparing 5 training-free statistical proxies (fanout, Pearson correlation, mutual information, conditional entropy reduction, homophily) against probe-based JRN (GBM and MLP) for predicting join utility across FK joins in the RelBench rel-f1 (Formula 1) schema. Uses relbench driver tasks and self-supervised tasks for races/constructors/circuits. Outputs per-join results, Spearman correlation matrix, Steiger's Z-test, and per-task analysis. Mini data includes FK join metadata, fanout statistics, correlation features, and JRN scores across 9 tables and 13 FK relationships.
